# Lecture Knowledge Walkthrough

## Lecture: Generic Practical Analysis

### ★★★ Absorbance and concentration calculation

Absorbance is proportional to concentration when the measurement stays within the calibrated linear range. A calibration curve converts measured absorbance into concentration by fitting a line, using the unknown sample absorbance, and applying any dilution factor.

Calculation Logic:

- Check that the unknown falls within the standard-curve range.
- Use the gradient and intercept to calculate concentration.
- Apply dilution factors after the curve-derived concentration is obtained.
- Report the final value with units and a short interpretation.

### ★★★ Graph and statistics selection

Graph choice follows the data type and experimental design. Continuous paired data require a paired comparison, independent continuous groups require an unpaired comparison, and more than two independent groups require an ANOVA-type comparison when assumptions are met.

Graph Logic:

- Use scatter or bar summaries only when the plotted statistic matches the measurement design.
- State the comparison being tested before choosing the statistical test.
- Treat non-parametric tests as rank-based alternatives when distribution assumptions are not met.
